rulings.Case Title: CITIZENS STATE BANK VS. BRETT K. LAMPORT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Hearing Date On Respondents Joint Motion To Vacate Sister State Judgment(S)

rulings.Case Title: FAST CAPITAL, LLC. ET AL VS. LA COSTA LOANS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 8, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANT LA COSTA LOANS, INC. MOTION TO STRIKE 4TH Amended COMPLAINT is moot. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: FAST CAPITAL, LLC. ET AL VS. LA COSTA LOANS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 8, 2020 line 2. DEFENDANT SS DREAMBUILDERS, LLC, CHONG HONG DEMURRER to 4TH Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend. The Court has previously sustained demurrers in this action with specific instructions regarding amendments necessary to address arguments raised by the demurrers. Amended pleadings failed to comply with the Court's instructions and no explanation for such failure is provided in the new pleading or in opposition to the demurrer. While the Fourth Amended Complaint is over 20 pages longer than the First Amended Complaint, the factual allegations regarding standing, contract-based and tort-based claims are insufficient. Plaintiff shall have the final opportunity to make factual allegations to support their claims. General conclusory statements are not sufficient. For any breach of contractual provisions the Plaintiff shall attach any written contracts and allege the terms of any oral contracts. For any claims of fraud and concealment factual allegations with requisite particularity (who, when, how, to whom, etc.) shall be alleged. Amendments shall also address November 20, 2018 ruling. All factual allegations and all amendments made pursuant to this ruling shall be in bold font. If factual allegations and amendments are again not made in bold font, Defendant may bring an ex-parte application to strike the non-complaint amended complaint. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: SAN DONG BEST INC. ET AL VS. GENERAL TSO KITCHEN, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 8, 2020 line 3. DEFENDANT DAVID TSAO DEMURRER to 4TH Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 10 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for breach of contract. While the meaning of the term "sold" as it s was used by that parties is apparently subject to dispute, for the purposes of the demurrer the Court is bound to accept Plaintiff's interpretation. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ZHEN ZHEN HU ET AL VS. GEORGE PAUL HE-SEMFIELD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 8, 2020 line 4. PLAINTIFF ZHEN ZHEN HU, RUI ZHUO SITU Notice Of Motion And Motion For Preliminary Injunction Hearing Required. All parties to be present. Plaintiffs' counsel to create a zoom meeting and provide a link to the court (Department501ContestTR@sftc.org) and to the defense counsel by 8:30 a.m. on October 8, 2020. The parties are ordered to meet and confer on rules regarding possible co-occupancy of the premises. Specifically, parties are to address items listed in reply on pages 9 and 10. There are 16 items in the bullet-point list. Parties to address items 5, 6, 7, 10, 11, 12, 13 and 15. Parties to submit a joint supplemental brief by no later than 8:30 a.m. on October 8, 2020 specifying the agreed upon solutions as to each point, if any; or listing their respective positions/proposed solutions for those points that cannot be agreed upon. Further parties are to meet and confer regarding the repairs in item 8 (loose tile, sink drainage, ventilation, and faucet). The parties should indicate in their joint brief whether the issues have been resolved; and whether they agreed on necessary repairs and the timelines for such repairs or list their respective positions regarding the repairs. Plaintiffs shall also provide copies of communications referenced in item 14 along with the joint brief. Motion for preliminary injunction regarding (1) cameras installed outside the front door to the unit currently occupied by the parties is GRANTED as follows: cameras may remain as long as they are directed away from the front door to the unit; (2) cameras inside the unit beyond the front door is MOOT as Defendants agreed that no cameras will be installed; (3) Hearing Required to address defendants' occupancy and use of the third bedroom and common areas. Among other issues to be addressed Defendants to be prepared to address why they cannot move in with their parents in the second unit; (4) HEARING REQUIRED to address specific items listed above. =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: VIOLETA SANCHEZ VS. JEZABEL MORALES ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 8, 2020 line 5. DEFENDANT JEZABEL MORALES DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT is OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently states a cause of action for unlawful detainer and is not uncertain. =(501/CFH)
